rationales.stimulation: He balks at the department-head role because it would bring policy changes he cannot control and disrupt the predictability of his current days.
rationales.security: What steadies him is knowing exactly what to prepare and what to expect, so the promotion reads primarily as a threat to routine and stability.
He checks the bank balance, pays the electricity bill early, sorts the exam papers into neat piles, and says he can finally breathe when there are 'no loose ends.'
He sticks with the same exam format because it works and feels good that his father's appointment is arranged in advance rather than left to a day-of scramble.
rationales.benevolence: He checks his timetable and coordinates with the front office so he can drive his father to the doctor's appointment.

rationales.achievement: He had flagged Section B's weak unit months earlier, put in extra time, and carefully documented summer packets, parent calls, and syllabus changes to improve next year.
rationales.security: He dwells on the relief of every gradebook cell being filled, every calculation double-checked, and every report card ready early instead of leaving loose ends.
On his first day off he still checks the news, bank app, and school portal, finds the empty grading spot disorienting, and immediately verifies the budget for the engagement gift.
He falls back into his usual lock-up routine, checks the stove knobs, and prepares for summer session by reviewing the roster and reusing last year's lesson plans because they already work.
His strongest reaction to Samir's request is that a failed shop would put the debt on his name and threaten the rent, car payments, and household stability he has to protect.
